items.title: Benjamin Netanyahu pays tribute to Lindsey Graham, "a great friend of Israel"
items.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu called Graham a "great friend of Israel." "In our recent meeting, I said, 'Lindsey is a great friend of Israel and a cherished friend of mine. We have no better friend than Lindsey.' Lindsey understood that the security of Israel and America are inseparable," he said in a statement released by his office. "He devoted his life to defending America, strengthening our alliance and standing up for the free world. Israel has lost one of its greatest friends." Our correspondent Noga Tarnopolsky tells us more.

items.title: World No. 1 defends Wimbledon title
items.description: Jannik Sinner is Wimbledon champion once again! The world No. 1 defeated Alexander Zverev 6-7, 7-6, 6-3, 6-4 in a thrilling four-set final to defend his title. The turning point came when Zverev slipped and appeared to injure his knee in the third set, allowing Sinner to seize control. The Italian kept his composure, sealed his fifth Grand Slam crown, and celebrated by collapsing onto Centre Court in delight. For Zverev, there was still some consolation as he'll rise to world No. 2 despite the defeat. Jean-Emile Jammine speaks with Italian tennis journalist, Ubaldo Scanagatta.

items.description: The Pakistani and Saudi Foreign Ministers exchanged views on the recent developments in the region and expressed deep concern over the escalations despite the signing of the Islamabad MoU

items.title: Spain edges Belgium and will face France in World Cup semifinal
items.description: Spain and France will meet Tuesday in Arlington, Texas, in a matchup anticipated for years. Neither team has lost at this year's World Cup.

items.title: Government intervenes as shipping shocks expose container vulnerability
items.description: The first outcome of the ₹10,000 crore container manufacturing scheme announced in Union Budget 2026-27 came on July 3, when an India-made EXIM container built by DCM Shriram Group was unveiled at Dadri for Maersk, which has placed a follow-on order for another 1,000 containers

items.title: WHO warns Sudan's cholera outbreak may worsen amid conflict and rains
items.description: The latest outbreak has claimed more than 100 lives so far and infected more than 1,300 others across several states.

items.title: How will Qatar’s Father Emir be remembered?
items.description: Former Emir Sheikh Hamad Bin Khalifa Al Thani has died

items.title: Blind bowler's dream threatened by NDIS funding cuts
items.description: A blind bowler from regional WA say his dreams of representing Australia are being swept away along with his NDIS funding.

items.title: Alvarez's 112th-minute goal helps lift Argentina past Switzerland 3-1 and into World Cup semifinals
items.description: Julian Alvarez sent defending champion Argentina into the World Cup semifinals with a long-range strike in the 112th minute against Switzerland.

items.title: T. rex could become most expensive fossil ever
items.description: A 67 million-year-old Tyrannosaurus rex will go on sale in New York with a pre-sale value of $30m.

items.title: How a viral seal called Neil caused mayhem in Australia
items.description: The five-year-old elephant seal attracted global attention during his recent time ashore in southern Tasmania.

items.title: Argentina cling to Messi in bid to break Europe’s World Cup grip
items.description: Reigning champions are the only non-European side left in the tournament and must overcome Switzerland to keep Lionel Messi’s dream alive. Leer más

items.title: NATO | Towards a more European alliance
items.description: Faced with external security challenges and internal divisions, especially under a sceptical Donald Trump administration, European members are preparing to play a larger role in keeping the alliance united and functional

items.title: Iraqi PM to meet US President Trump in Washington to deepen strategic ties
items.description: Oil and gas deals are expected ⁠to be signed as part of a broader push ​for economic, trade and investment cooperation.

items.title: Typhoon Bavi weakens but still brings strong winds, rain to China
items.description: Strong winds and heavy rain are expected to impact many eastern and northeastern Chinese cities on Sunday (July 12) and Monday (July 13), China's National Meteorological Centre said

items.title: Spain ex-PM draws criticism with ‘xenophobic’ remark on French team
items.description: The comment, published in an opinion piece in Spanish online news site El Debate, came as Spain prepares to face France in a blockbuster FIFA World Cup semi-final

items.title: Japanese sake brewery to make foray into Indian market
items.description: Takahashi Shouten, based in the city of Yame in Fukuoka Prefecture, launched a subsidiary in India in February.

items.title: South Africa World Cup midfielder Adams dies aged 25
items.description: South Africa and Mamelodi Sundowns midfielder Jayden Adams dies at the age of 25, just weeks after featuring for his country at the World Cup.

items.title: Stories that caught our eye: July 3 to 10
items.description: A selection of the stories that caught our eye over the last seven days in Argentina. Leer más

items.title: Merz: Germany agreed to buy Tomahawk missiles at NATO summit
items.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has informed the Bundestag parliament of a deal to buy US Tomahawk cruise missiles and station them in Germany. He said this closed a "important strategic ‌gap ​in our defense."

items.title: Iran expands attacks on Gulf states after intense US strikes
items.description: Iran reported strikes Sunday evening on two of its southern islands while Kuwait, where Tehran has repeatedly targeted US installations, said border posts and an offshore oil platform had been attacked. The as-yet unclaimed strikes came hours after Tehran and WashingtonStrait of Hormuz, offering contradicting statements as to whether the vital waterway was open to traffic.

items.title: Gas queues grow as Ukraine targets Russia's fuel supply
items.description: Attacks by Ukraine have triggered a fuel crisis in Russia. Reports from across Russia show how residents are dealing with the disruptions in their daily lives.

items.title: Japan to push its massive pension fund to boost alternative investments, Nikkei says
DR Congo says Ebola outbreak yet to peak as virus spreads to new areas
items.description: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DR Congo) remains in a "very active" phase and has yet to peak, health authorities said Thursday

items.title: Mauritania frees lawmakers convicted over discrimination claims
items.description: Mauritania's President Mohamed Ould Cheikh El Ghazouani has pardoned two opposition lawmakers who were sentenced to four years in prison after accusing him of racial discrimination against Black Mauritanians and the descendants of enslaved people.

items.title: How a Jewish heir is taking on Germany's far-right AfD
items.description: Heavily supported in eastern states, the Alternative for Germany is using a GDR cultural icon, the Schwalbe moped, in its campaigning. Now, the Jewish heir of the manufacturer is fighting to protect his family's legacy.

items.title: Nine dead as Russia, Ukraine exchange strikes
items.description: The strikes came a day before Ukraine's allies were set to meet in Paris for talks on pressuring Russia to end its more than four-year war

items.title: Te Deum: Archbishop urges end to 'insults and cruelty' as Milei watches on
items.description: In what some saw as a pointed rebuke to President Javier Milei, Archbishop of Buenos Aires Jorge García Cuerva calls on leaders to “stop disparaging others, regardless of their political party,” as he urges greater “care for the poor, the sick and the disabled” in annual ‘Te Deum’ speech. Leer más

items.title: Trump says US will 'completely decimate' Iran if assassinated
items.description: President Donald Trump said Friday the United States would "completely decimate" Iran if it attempted or succeeded in assassinating the sitting president.

items.title: Ukraine's Zelensky proposes new PM in Ukraine government reshuffle
items.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky said on Sunday he had proposed replacing Prime Minister Yulia Svyrydenko as part of a broader government reshuffle aimed at implementing an "updated political strategy".

items.title: Spain wildfire: Firefighters gain ground as Almería evacuees return home
items.description: Firefighters gained the upper hand on the deadly Almería wildfire after days of battling the flames, as the blaze that killed 12 people and burned 66 square kilometres began to ease, allowing some of the nearly 1,500 evacuees to return home.

items.title: Why the World Cup can be good for your mental health
items.description: The World Cup can offer more than just entertainment, it can also benefit your mental well-being. DW explains why.

items.title: British PM Rishi Sunak avoids wipeout in key local elections
items.description: By-elections were seen as an indicator of the two main parties' prospects at a time when voters are struggling with high inflation, strikes and rising mortgage rates.

items.title: Toyota eyes lunar rover powered by regenerative fuel-cell tech
items.description: Toyota has teamed up with JAXA since 2019 to develop the manned lunar rover — which it dubbed the Lunar Cruiser — that they hope can be put on the moon in 2029.

items.title: US Democrat Ro Khanna says he was detained by armed Israeli settlers
items.description: Khanna, 49, was visiting the occupied West Bank when he was held for 90 minutes, he says.

items.title: Argentina fans erupt as Messi's men reach World Cup semifinal
items.description: Argentina fans celebrate in Buenos Aires after a dramatic 3-1 extra-time win over ten-man Switzerland sends the defending champions into the World Cup semifinals.

items.title: Opposition rallies against Tshisekedi's third-term push
items.description: Political tensions are rising in the Democratic Republic of Congo as opposition leaders demand President Félix Tshisekedi's resignation and call for nationwide protests over proposed constitutional changes that could extend his time in power.

items.title: Mitch McConnell releases health update, says he is ‘regaining’ strength
items.description: The US Republican senator has been absent from Congress during a weeks-long hospitalisation, spurring party concern.

items.title: Is the Strait of Hormuz still Iran's trump card?
items.description: Iran's latest attacks show it can easily disrupt shipping, energy markets and draw in Gulf neighbors. DW asks how much leverage Tehran really has over Washington and if its high-risk strategy could backfire.

items.title: Qatar’s former emir remembered as ‘a man of vision and courage’
items.description: US diplomat Adam Ereli remembers Qatar’s former Emir Sheikh as a personable and charismatic leader.

items.title: Can ageless Lionel Messi keep delivering for Argentina at World Cup?
items.description: Aside from their diminutive genius, Argentina's most potent weapon is their remarkable camaraderie. The defending champions are now unbeaten in 11 World Cup games and the nation is dreaming of a fourth world title. Leer más

items.title: Commentary: Micron’s massive profits are a guarantee of trouble
items.description: Chip scarcity is forcing up memory prices and making the likes of Micron and SK Hynix astonishingly profitable. That's a guarantee of trouble, says Chris Bryant for Bloomberg Opinion.

items.title: US Senator Lindsey Graham dies
items.description: The South Carolina politician and staunch ally of Donald Trump died aged 71 after ​a brief and sudden illness, his office said. The hawkish senator backed the 2003 invasion of Iraq and the more recent war with Iran.

items.title: Mayor of Berlin says he won't run for reelection
items.description: Berlin Mayor Kai Wegner withdrew from the reelection race this fall. His decision to play tennis during the power outage in January was among missteps that has dented his reputation.

items.title: Indian tourists among 15 killed as speedboat capsizes in Vietnam
items.description: Local media say 21 people were saved after the boat overturned in rough seas near an island in south Vietnam.

items.title: Hungary public media reform begins with black screen apology
items.description: People tuning in to public service media in Hungary on Tuesday were greeted by a black screen and an apology. Peter Magyar has begun dismantling one of Viktor Orban's most potent tools of power.

items.title: Iran's late Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei laid to rest in Mashhad
items.description: Thousands of mourners performed funeral prayers Thursday on the final day of ceremonies for Iran's late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

items.title: Argentina’s political parties look for ways to avoid extremes
items.description: With next year’s election set to offer a referendum on President Javier Milei’s reformist agenda, some influential business leaders and opposition figures are discussing the possibility that violent shifts of policy are coming to an end – regardless of who prevails in the 2027 vote. Leer más

items.title: South African president welcomed by Macron at start of visit to France
items.description: Cyril Ramaphosa will also co-chair a UNESCO meeting on education and take part in the 110th anniversary of the Battle of Delville Wood.
